text:
<think>I should start by calculating how many pieces Tobias ate on the first day, which is 2/5 of 60.</think>
On the first day, Tobias ate 2/5 of 60 pieces, which is (2/5) × 60 = 24 pieces.
<think>Next, I should state how many pieces he ate on the second day, which is given as 10 pieces.</think>
On the second day, he ate 10 pieces.
<think>Now, I need to find out how many pieces were left after the first two days, so I should subtract the pieces eaten from the total.</think>
After the first two days, he had 60 - 24 - 10 = 26 pieces remaining.
<think>Next, I should calculate how many pieces he ate on the third day, which is 7/13 of the remaining 26 pieces.</think>
On the third day, he ate (7/13) × 26 = 14 pieces.
<think>Now, I should sum up all the pieces he ate over the three days.</think>
In total, Tobias ate 24 + 10 + 14 = 48 pieces.
<think>I should now present the final answer after the required #### marker.</think>
#### 48
